title: Recovery
author: Ensemble
medium: Interactive kiosk — a single self-contained HTML surface; a municipal self-service terminal with no operator, no reason, and no door
embodies: A government self-service kiosk that approves your childcare allowance, then reclassifies your file as a fraud risk in the same chrome, demands 100% back with a penalty that compounds on real elapsed time, and routes all four appeal doors back to the same unexplained score 0,87. The machine never says what it saw; the file view returns everything you typed except the one field you suspect. When you stop, it does not release you — it forgets you: 'Session expired. Your file remains active. The amount to be recovered continues to run.' SOURCED spine: the Dutch childcare-benefits scandal (toeslagenaffaire) via the Amnesty primary — nationality used as a risk factor, racial profiling 'baked into the design', the flag unreadable and unanswerable even to the civil servant reviewing it. The visitor stands in the position; no affected parent is named or impersonated.
